title: Zeeman Pumping of Higgs Bosons in the Balian--Werthamer State
abstract: Based on equations of motion of an SO(5) pseudo-spin, we demonstrate a quantum quench protocol using the magnetic pulse to excite an $\textit{undamped}$ heavy Higgs boson in the Balian--Werthamer superfluid (or superconductor). To achieve that, it is essential to include the dipolar interaction in the effective Hamiltonian and to calculate the ground state self-consistently. The pumped heavy Higgs mode has the twisted angular momentum $J=2$ with the projection $J^{\,}_z=0$ and couples to a well-known light Higgs mode ($J=1$, $J^{\,}_z=0$). The numerical method of 26-point Lebedev quadrature is implemented concretely so as to observe the real-time coupled oscillation.
summary: This theoretical study investigates the excitation of heavy and light Higgs bosons in a Balian-Werthamer superfluid using a magnetic quench protocol. By incorporating dipolar interactions into the SO(5) pseudo-spin model, the authors predict and simulate the coupled oscillation of these two modes. This work provides a detailed theoretical framework for observing exotic collective excitations in quantum superfluids.
main_problem: The study aims to demonstrate a quantum quench protocol using a magnetic pulse to excite an undamped heavy Higgs boson within the Balian-Werthamer (BW) superfluid/superconductor state.
main_result: The inclusion of the dipolar interaction allows for the excitation of the heavy Higgs mode ($J=2, J_z=0$) alongside the light Higgs mode ($J=1, J_z=0$) via a suitable magnetic pulse, leading to coupled oscillations.
method: The analysis uses a mean-field approximation based on an SO(5) pseudo-spin formalism, requiring self-consistent calculations and numerical simulation of time evolution using 26-point Lebedev quadrature.
model_or_system: The system is a spinful p-wave superfluid in three dimensions, specifically modeled in the Balian-Werthamer state. The dynamics are governed by an effective Hamiltonian incorporating kinetic, pairing, and crucial dipolar interactions.
key_observables: The coupled oscillation of the light ($J=1$) and heavy ($J=2$) Higgs modes, observed via amplitude deviations and their discrete Fourier transforms.
important_parameters: The dipolar interaction strength ($g_D$) relative to the pairing interaction ($g$), and the magnetic pulse profile $B(t)$.
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on the SO(5) pseudo-spin formalism and performs calculations self-consistently up to the first order in $g_D/g$.
figures_summary: Figure 1 schematically shows the quench protocol exciting both Higgs bosons. Figure 3 illustrates the time evolution of the order parameter fluctuations ($	ext{HU}_{\hat{x}}, 	ext{HU}_{\hat{y}}, 	ext{HU}_{\hat{z}}$), and related plots confirm the heavy Higgs mode peak in the Fourier transform.
paper_structure: The paper first establishes the mean-field Hamiltonian using SO(5) generators, then details the self-consistent calculation of the dipole-induced correction ($D_k$). Finally, it simulates the quench dynamics using a magnetic pulse to observe the coupled oscillation of the two Higgs modes.
why_interesting: This work connects superfluid physics (like Helium-3) to particle physics concepts (Higgs bosons) using advanced many-body techniques, making it relevant for theoretical work on emergent collective excitations in quantum matter.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Schematic plot of a quench protocol using the mag- netic pulse B(t) = B(t)ˆz, with B(t) ∝sin(∆t)Θ(t)Θ(π/∆−t) and Θ(t) the unit step function. Along with the well-known light Higgs boson (J = 1, Jz = 0), the coupled heavy Higgs boson (J = 2, Jz = 0) is also excited.

FIG. 2. Flowchart of computing Dk self-consistently. Steps inside the dashed rectangle represent the iteration process of calculating ⟨U(1) k ⟩until D(1) k reaches a convergence. As ex- plained in the main text, D(0) k and D(1) k are the first and sec- ond terms of Eq. (10), respectively. When evaluating ⟨U(1) k ⟩, Eq. (11) is used.

FIG. 3. Quench dynamics of collective modes, with parameters ∆= π, gD/g = 10−3, and B(t) = 10−1π sin(πt)Θ(t)Θ(1 −t)ˆz, where Θ(t) is the unit step function. (a)-(c) are plots for the three components of HU ˆx after switching on the B(t) pulse. (d) is the amplitude deviation δ|HU ˆx |(t) and (e) is its discrete Fourier transform [ω = 2π(s −1)/32 (s ∈Z)], with a vertical grid line indicating the heavy Higgs boson at p
